## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about your post registration asset set. So what in the world is a post registration asset? Well, it's the pages and elements that you need to create to support the process of getting people to attend your webinar. And the asset set is that combination of those. All right. So let's talk about each element of that you need to build to bring people, the most people who register for your webinar into actually attending your webinar. So pages, web pages. So you're going to need to thank you page. This begins that push to attend. So prior to this, all of your pages that are promoting your webinar have been to promote them signing up for the webinar. Now at the minute they sign up, we shift our communication from, Hey, here's this cool thing you ought to sign up to, Hey, here's this really cool thing you should definitely attend. And so we're making that shift with this first page, the thank you page. Then we have the bonding page. That's a web page that builds their relationship with you. I talk more about that in a separate video where I actually gave you the description of what could should actually be in that video. And the purpose of the bonding page is to generate trust in you. So they attend. And then we've got the transformational content pages. I recommend three. You may have two, you may have five, but these are specifically designed to teach them concepts related to your webinar's topic and begin creating the transformation that you promised in the webinar so that they'll want to come to the webinar to complete that transformation. Here, your purpose is to tell them that you have the info that they need, that you can be relied upon to deliver that info and that it's worth their time to attend. All right. Now we're going to move from web pages on to Facebook. The next asset you're going to build is a Facebook group specifically for your webinar. In this Facebook group, you're building relationships, community, and driving them to these other assets. And so it's a place where if people are not paying attention to their email or people prefer to communicate on Facebook, and it's certainly the only place that they're going to get that community feel, they're getting that from this Facebook group. Also, you're going to build a Facebook asset of a messenger campaign here that they go into once they sign up. And this reminds them of the webinar and drives them to these other assets. Now let's turn to emails and other reminders. So you've got your autoresponder sequence, which purposes to remind them and

## [03:03 - 06:05]

drive them to the other assets. And then you've got your webinar provider sequence. And this is reminders and giving them that webinar link. Now, why would we want to use both? There are many people who say, Oh, you shouldn't be sending both that double counts and everything. Well, there's a couple of things. The first one is webinar provider emails usually get higher click rates because they are what we call transactional emails. So the technical definition of a transactional email is an email that is necessary for them to be able to complete the transaction. For example, if you buy something that's a download, the transactional email is that download link. And so they usually have a far higher click rate. And so they usually have a far higher deliverability rate. Since webinar provider emails are usually happening the moment someone opts in and just before the webinar, they tend to get a lot of clicks, especially that one that happens just before the webinar. Consequently, they have a very high deliverability. And so if your autoresponder sequence is going to their promotions tab, there's a high higher likelihood that the webinar provider sequence is going to actually land in their inbox, be seen and get clicked. That's the reason why we do both to support your webinar. And then we're also going to want to build in an SMS, which is texting reminder. This gives them a last minute reminder. You're only really going to send out one. Just reminding them, Hey, this thing is starting now. All right. Now, we also have a response mechanism where they can respond back to you and tell you what it is that they want. This is a questionnaire, which finds out what's most important to them and gets their language.
